domain.name: Work Management
domain.description: Task, project, and workflow management for cross-functional work: assignments with owners and due dates, dependencies, statuses, dashboards, automations, and goal/OKR tracking. Bought by PMOs, BizOps, marketing, and any team running structured work that isn't engineering-specific. Distinct from SPM (top-down portfolio investment decision, CIO/EPMO buyer), from PSA (services billing/utilization for revenue-generating projects), and from dedicated software-engineering sprint tooling (developer-specific dev workflow).
domain.catalog_tagline: Run every team's projects, tasks, and goals in one place, from first request to finished work.
domain.catalog_description:
Bring structured work out of scattered spreadsheets and inboxes. Create projects, break them into assignable tasks with owners and due dates, map dependencies, and watch progress on boards, timelines, and dashboards that update as the work moves.

Set team objectives and key results, then connect them to the tasks that move them so progress rolls up automatically. Standardize repeatable work with templates, and let automations handle status changes, reminders, and routing.

Built for operations, marketing, and any team running cross-functional work that needs one shared view of who is doing what, by when.

modules.name: Team-Execution Goals and OKRs
modules.description: Team-execution OKR tracking surface: objectives with key results that link to work items for automatic progress rollup, weekly check-in cadences, scoring, and closure. Deploys alongside the task-execution module for full integration, or standalone with a thin embedded work-item shell for KR linking.

modules.name: Work Request Intake
modules.description: Structured request-intake surface: form definitions, form submissions, routing rules. Pre-task surface that converts a submission into a work_item in the target TASK-EXEC project. Backs the WORK-INTAKE-FORMS capability with entities. Vendor reference: Asana Forms, Monday Forms, ClickUp Forms, Workfront Request Queues, Smartsheet Forms.

modules.name: Task and Project Execution
modules.description: Cross-functional task and project execution surface: work items with owners, due dates, dependencies, statuses, and assignments; project containers with timelines, dashboards, and board views; user-authored automation rules that fire on item state changes. The deployable substrate every team-based work-management product orbits.

data_objects.name: okr_check_ins
data_objects.description: Periodic status update on an okr_objective or key_result during the active cycle. Cadence-of-record (weekly/bi-weekly) for OKR programs. May discuss individual performance, flagged as personal content.

data_objects.name: okr_key_results
data_objects.description: Measurable result attached to an okr_objective. The unit of scoring on OKR programs, vendors universally model KR as first-class (Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Workfront).

data_objects.name: okr_objectives
data_objects.description: Hierarchical objective with measurable key results, weighted progress rollup from child objectives or linked work_items, owner accountability, and cadence (quarterly/annual). Mastered by three distinct domains: WORK-MGMT (team-level execution OKRs), SPM (strategic portfolio OKRs), TALENT-MGMT (individual performance-management OKRs). Same primitive, three different lifecycles and review processes, canonical Signal-1 multi-master.

data_objects.name: proofing_annotations
data_objects.description: Markup comment placed on a region of a proof during a proofing session, with the reviewer, location, and resolution state.

data_objects.name: proofing_sessions
data_objects.description: Review-and-approval cycle on a creative asset or document attached to a work item. Reviewers mark up the proof and decide approve or request-changes; backs the creative-review capability.

data_objects.name: work_approval_chains
data_objects.description: Ordered or parallel set of work_approval_steps gating a work_item or work_project transition. Backs the APPROVAL-WORKFLOW capability (cross-cutting; this is the WORK-MGMT realization).

data_objects.name: work_approval_steps
data_objects.description: Single approval gate on a work_item or work_project, performed by a designated approver. Belongs to a work_approval_chain. Per-step states: pending / approved / rejected / expired.

data_objects.name: work_automations
data_objects.description: Trigger-action rule defined per board/project: status change, due date, assignment, form submission as triggers; multi-step actions with conditions, time delays, and external integrations. The user-authored behavior layer on top of the data primitives.

data_objects.name: work_custom_field_values
data_objects.description: Per-work_item value for a work_custom_field. Junction-style master: tuple (work_item, work_custom_field, value). Monday column_values, Asana custom_field_values, Smartsheet cells.

data_objects.name: work_custom_fields
data_objects.description: User-defined field attached to a work_project or workspace, typed (text, number, date, select, etc.). Applies to all work_items in scope. Monday columns / Asana custom fields / ClickUp custom fields / Workfront / Smartsheet column types.

data_objects.name: work_dashboards
data_objects.description: Saved, shareable cross-project dashboard composed of configurable widgets that summarize work progress, workload, and goal status. Backs the cross-project dashboards capability.

data_objects.name: work_dependencies
data_objects.description: Typed dependency between two work_items: predecessor/successor with type (FS finish-to-start, SS start-to-start, FF finish-to-finish, SF start-to-finish). Backs the WORK-DEPS-SCHED capability. Modeled by Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Workfront, Smartsheet.

data_objects.name: work_form_submissions
data_objects.description: Submitted instance of a work_form. Carries field values, submitter, timestamp. Converts to a work_item via routing rules. May contain personal content from form fields (requester name/email).

data_objects.name: work_forms
data_objects.description: Structured intake form definition: schema (fields, validation), target project, routing rules. Asana Forms, Monday Forms, ClickUp Forms, Workfront Request Queues, Smartsheet Forms.

data_objects.name: work_goal_links
data_objects.description: Contribution link between a work item or project and an okr_objective so that goal progress can roll up from the work that drives it.

data_objects.name: work_item_attachments
data_objects.description: File or external link attached to a work_item. May reference embedded content or platform-managed storage.

data_objects.name: work_item_comments
data_objects.description: Threaded comment on a work_item. Asana stories / Monday updates / ClickUp comments / Workfront updates / Smartsheet conversations. May include personal or sensitive content.

data_objects.name: work_item_tags
data_objects.description: Junction master between work_items and work_tags. Carries no qualifier of its own; existence asserts the tag is applied.

data_objects.name: work_items
data_objects.description: Atomic primitive in a work-management platform: task / item / card with owner, due date, status, priority, dependencies, subtasks, attachments, and comments. Same shape regardless of platform-specific terminology (task, item, row, card).

data_objects.name: work_milestones
data_objects.description: Zero-duration gate within a work_project marking a meaningful transition. Distinct from a regular work_item by gate semantics (passed/missed). Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Workfront, Smartsheet all model.

data_objects.name: work_portfolios
data_objects.description: Team-level grouping of related projects for cross-project status roll-up. Distinct from the top-down strategic portfolio mastered in the strategic-portfolio domain.

data_objects.name: work_project_templates
data_objects.description: Reusable work_project blueprint with seeded sections, items, custom fields, and automations. Universal across all 5 flagships; central to enterprise rollout patterns.

data_objects.name: work_projects
data_objects.description: Container of work_items, regardless of platform-specific terminology (project, board, sheet, space/list). Has timeline, status, owner, members, dashboards, and embedded views.

data_objects.name: work_sections
data_objects.description: Container within a work_project that groups work_items for board/list views. Asana sections / Monday groups / ClickUp statuses-as-columns / Workfront statuses / Smartsheet parent rows.

data_objects.name: work_status_updates
data_objects.description: Logged status-change event on a work item or project capturing the prior status, the new status, who changed it, and when.

data_objects.name: work_statuses
data_objects.description: Configurable status value in a project's or team's status taxonomy, with its category (not started, in progress, done) and display order.

data_objects.name: work_tags
data_objects.description: Free-form label attached to work_items for cross-cutting categorization (priority, theme, status). Many-to-many to work_items via work_item_tags.

data_objects.name: work_task_templates
data_objects.description: Reusable work_item blueprint with seeded assignee role, due-date offset, subtasks, and custom field defaults. Used for recurring or templated work creation.

data_objects.name: work_time_entries
data_objects.description: Non-billable time logged against a work item for effort tracking and capacity reporting. Billable worklog with rate, utilization, and invoice stays mastered in the services-billing domain and is consumed, not re-mastered, here.

data_objects.name: work_user_workloads
data_objects.description: Per-user, per-period sum of allocated effort on work_items, with capacity ceiling. Backs the WORK-CAPACITY capability. Continuous-numeric, not state-driven. May expose individual workload.

data_objects.name: work_views
data_objects.description: Saved, named, shareable board / list / timeline / calendar view of work items with persisted filters, grouping, and sort. A configuration record, not a per-user UI toggle.

system_skills.description: Plan projects and break them into assignable tasks with owners, due dates, and statuses; map dependencies and milestones on boards, lists, calendars, and timelines; and show who is doing what by when across teams. Set objectives and key results, link them to the work that moves them, and log check-ins so progress rolls up. Stand up request intake forms and route submissions, run approval and proofing reviews, automate status changes and reminders, balance workload against capacity, and report on cross-project dashboards.
system_skills.trigger_keywords: work management, project tracker, task tracker, to-do list, kanban, swimlane, Gantt, roadmap, sprint backlog, burndown, milestone tracking, dependency mapping, ticket queue, request queue, work intake, OKR, KPI, goal tracking, timesheets, resource allocation, capacity planning, workload balancing, creative markup, proofing, approval workflow, status rollup, cross-project dashboards, PMO, BizOps, marketing ops, operations team
